rulings.Case Title: FLICK, INC. VS. MING CHAO LIU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motions And Motions To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Respondent Ming Chao Liu And Supporting Memo
rulings.Rulings: "Matter on the Discovery calendar for Monday, December 16, 2024, Line 1. PETITIONER FLICK, INC.'s Motions To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Respondent Ming Chao Liu And Supporting Memo. Petitioner Flick Inc.'s Motions To Compel Answers To Interrogatories And Production Of Documents From Respondent Ming Chao Liu is GRANTED. Petitioner's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART. Good cause appearing, the court ORDERS Respondent Ming Chao Liu: (1) to provide Code compliant responses to Flick's First Set of Special Interrogatories, and (2) provide a Code compliant response including an appropriate document production, to Flick's First Requests for Production of Documents. Complete responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. The court cannot find Respondent Ming Chao Liu's response (or non-response) to the subject discovery requests and instant motion substantially justified. The court ORDERS Respondent Ming Chao Liu to pay to Flick, Inc. $2,300 as reasonable sanctions. Payment within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. ** Department 301's 12/16/24 discovery and law and motion calendars will both be called at 10:00 a.m.** For the 10:00 a.m. Discovery calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ).

rulings.Case Title: TIAN ZHANG VS. APEX CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion Calendar for December 16, 2024, line 1. DEFENDANT STEPHEN TONG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. Defendant Stephen Tong's Motion to Quash Service or Stay or Dismiss to which no opposition was filed is DENIED. The motion has not been served on all parties (including but not limited to Jonathan Padilla, Wing See Ng and Bosco Lai) to the action and judgment. (See Proof of Service, filed Nov. 8, 2024.) Even if service of the motion were proper, the complaint and summons were "duly served" when Tong made a general appearance in the action. (See CCP 410.50(a) ["[a] general appearance by a party is equivalent to personal service of summons on such party."]; see, e.g., Motion to Set Aside, filed Aug. 18, 2024; Case Management Statement, filed Nov. 8, 2024; Motion to Quash, filed Nov. 8, 2024.) Nor does the record on this motion support an order staying or dismissing the action. ** Department 301's 12/16/24 discovery and law and motion calendars will both be called at 10:00 a.m.** For the 10:00 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/JMQ)
